rss.channel:
# 🇱🇰 This Week in Sri Lanka https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/README.md Generated by gpt-5 from 163 English News Articles published between 2026-04-28 and 2026-05-05. <<<<<<< HEAD <<<<<<< HEAD <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000
Sri Lanka and the Maldives signed seven agreements as leaders marked 61 years of diplomatic ties in Colombo. The pacts covered counter‑terrorism, defence education, tourism, youth and sports cooperation, archival collaboration, and teacher training, while the sides also advanced discussions on business facilitation and financial-sector presence, including the possible entry of a Maldivian bank. The visiting delegation joined a business forum focused on investment, maritime connectivity and value‑added fisheries, areas singled out for commercial expansion alongside twin‑destination travel. Reciprocal land allocations for permanent chancery sites were announced, signalling institutional depth to the relationship beyond protocol. The two sides also noted shared Indian Ocean security interests and the economic linkages created by air links and hospitality flows, positioning the new accords as frameworks for execution rather than stand‑alone communiqués. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530
Sri Lanka and the Maldives signed seven agreements as leaders marked 61 years of diplomatic ties in Colombo. The pacts covered counter‑terrorism, defence education, tourism, youth and sports cooperation, archival collaboration, and teacher training, while the sides also advanced discussions on business facilitation and financial-sector presence, including the possible entry of a Maldivian bank. The visiting delegation joined a business forum focused on investment, maritime connectivity and value‑added fisheries, areas singled out for commercial expansion alongside twin‑destination travel. Reciprocal land allocations for permanent chancery sites were announced, signalling institutional depth to the relationship beyond protocol. The two sides also noted shared Indian Ocean security interests and the economic linkages created by air links and hospitality flows, positioning the new accords as frameworks for execution rather than stand‑alone communiqués. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#1-sri-lanka-and-the-maldives-signed-seven-agreements-as-leaders-marked-61-years-of-diplomatic-ties-in-colombo
Parliament received a Value Added Tax amendment bill that widens the base and taxes cross‑border digital services from July 2026. The draft lowers the registration threshold to Rs. 36 million per year and Rs. 9 million per period, pulling more small and mid‑sized traders into the net compared to the current Rs. 60 million and Rs. 15 million levels. Non‑resident platforms supplying electronically to users in the country must register and remit, while business‑to‑business transactions remain creditable subject to documentation. The rate on financial services rises to 20.5% from 18%, and retailers face mandated secure point‑of‑sale systems and stiffer non‑compliance penalties, with maximum fines moving to Rs. 1 million from Rs. 25,000. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#2-parliament-received-a-value-added-tax-amendment-bill-that-widens-the-base-and-taxes-crossborder-digital-services-from-july-2026
National Development Bank disclosed a fraud of about Rs. 13.2 billion as the Central Bank tightened reconciliation rules for licensed institutions. Following the bank’s market filings in early April and a subsequent downgrade of its national long‑term rating by Fitch, the monetary authority on 16 April issued a directive highlighting weaknesses in control environments and board oversight, and flagged risks to the reliability of reported figures. The notice requires faster, better‑evidenced reconciliations and elevates accountability for delays, complementing existing supervisory tools. The financial institution told investors it has suspended implicated staff, secured records, and commissioned an independent forensic review to report directly to the regulator. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#3-national-development-bank-disclosed-a-fraud-of-about-rs-132-billion-as-the-central-bank-tightened-reconciliation-rules-for-licensed-institutions
Nations Trust Bank completed the migration of HSBC Sri Lanka’s retail banking portfolio with effect from 1 May 2026. The transition absorbed roughly 200,000 customer accounts across deposits, cards, loans and wealth tiers, added seven branches including several private‑banking locations, and took the network footprint to 96 outlets nationwide. Staff of the transferred franchise joined the acquirer, with extended service hours and dedicated hotlines offered during the cutover to stabilise client access. The move follows formal approvals and is positioned as a scale and capability expansion in a market where capital‑light franchises have leaned into digital and affluent banking. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#4-nations-trust-bank-completed-the-migration-of-hsbc-sri-lankas-retail-banking-portfolio-with-effect-from-1-may-2026
A parliamentary finance panel reviewed a new insolvency and restructuring bill aimed at replacing liquidation‑led regimes. The proposal introduces court‑supervised moratoria, creditor voting on plans that bind all classes once thresholds are met, and priority finance to support viable turnarounds, in contrast to fragmented statutes dating to the 1800s and 1939. Stakeholders flagged tax clarity, priority of State claims, interactions with parate powers, and court capacity as implementation risks, with committee‑stage amendments signalled on definitional and operational gaps before debate. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#5-a-parliamentary-finance-panel-reviewed-a-new-insolvency-and-restructuring-bill-aimed-at-replacing-liquidationled-regimes
Dialog Axiata reported surpassing 1,000 live fifth‑generation mobile sites ahead of 2026 rollout targets set by the regulator. The expansion followed a commercial launch footprint of over 220 sites and more than 1.5 million compatible users, and included nationwide district coverage and an entry‑level 5G handset at LKR 35,999 to broaden access. The operator said the grid supports future enterprise and consumer use cases beyond enhanced mobile broadband. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#6-dialog-axiata-reported-surpassing-1000-live-fifthgeneration-mobile-sites-ahead-of-2026-rollout-targets-set-by-the-regulator
A visiting Russian health delegation began a three‑day programme including talks with health and foreign ministries and site visits in Colombo. The agenda featured discussions on pharmaceuticals, equipment and specialist training, as well as engagements at the national tertiary hospital and a medical faculty to explore academic exchanges. The trip aligned with prior technical cooperation and health‑sector modernisation priorities. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#7-a-visiting-russian-health-delegation-began-a-threeday-programme-including-talks-with-health-and-foreign-ministries-and-site-visits-in-colombo
Officials received a report outlining how to enable overseas voting for more than 1.5 million citizens abroad. The submission categorised eligible groups such as migrant workers, students and dual citizens, and set options for registration, campaigning rules, and balloting via missions, post or secure online channels. The ministry said the proposals will go to Cabinet and then to wider consultation, with advanced and mobile voting for essential domestic workers identified as an additional policy track. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#8-officials-received-a-report-outlining-how-to-enable-overseas-voting-for-more-than-15-million-citizens-abroad
Cabinet cleared rules expanding the Central Bank’s power to collect statistics from external parties and to sanction non‑compliance. The gazetted framework under the 2023 central banking law defines reporting scopes, confidentiality, formats, and administrative actions, and will be tabled in Parliament. The Cabinet also approved laying the institution’s 2025 financial statements and operational report before the legislature following audit by the Auditor General. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#9-cabinet-cleared-rules-expanding-the-central-banks-power-to-collect-statistics-from-external-parties-and-to-sanction-noncompliance
IFC announced financing for a 100‑megawatt utility‑scale solar facility to accelerate the renewable buildout. The package, partly in local currency via the IDA Private Sector Window, supports a grid‑connected plant expected to generate around 220 GWh annually and create several thousand jobs during implementation, with provision for future storage. Advisory assistance will cover engineering, grid resilience, cybersecurity and sustainability practices to de‑risk replication. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#10-ifc-announced-financing-for-a-100megawatt-utilityscale-solar-facility-to-accelerate-the-renewable-buildout
Janashakthi Limited listed 500 million shares on the Main Board after a Rs. 5 billion initial public offering. The issue, the largest flotation in about 14 years and three times oversubscribed, raises capital for expansion in insurance, microfinance and non‑bank financing, debt reduction, and selective regional moves. The group said listing enhances access to capital markets while widening the shareholder base in a recovering economy. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#11-janashakthi-limited-listed-500-million-shares-on-the-main-board-after-a-rs-5-billion-initial-public-offering
The National Chamber of Exporters launched a nationally accredited Certificate of Ethical Trading endorsed by the standards authority. The upgraded programme, first introduced in 2019 and now aligned with the Sri Lanka Accreditation Board, formalises assessments of governance, labour practices, environmental performance and transparency to meet buyer requirements. Over 100 firms have been certified, with the chamber saying accreditation strengthens credibility in markets where due‑diligence regimes are tightening. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#12-the-national-chamber-of-exporters-launched-a-nationally-accredited-certificate-of-ethical-trading-endorsed-by-the-standards-authority
A new Sri Lanka–Nepal Business Council was inaugurated in Colombo to deepen trade and investment links. The platform brings together companies and officials to promote sector‑specific cooperation in tourism, SMEs and handicrafts, facilitate delegations, and push for improvements in air connectivity. Leadership roles were assigned and a digital portal launched to share information and support matchmaking between firms. <<<<<<< HEAD Tue, 05 May 2026 11:53:01 +0000 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:51:36 +0530 >>>>>>> d231072 (Update README and broadsheet styles; adjust article color coding) https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#13-a-new-sri-lankanepal-business-council-was-inaugurated-in-colombo-to-deepen-trade-and-investment-links ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:52:59 +0530 ======= Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 >>>>>>> 773e9f6 (Update README and RSS feed for May 5, 2026; adjust article block rendering in Broadsheet mixin)
Sri Lanka and the Maldives signed seven cooperation agreements covering defence, education, tourism, health and archives. The instruments were exchanged at the Presidential Secretariat in Colombo during the State visit of President Mohamed Muizzu, marking 61 years since formal ties were established in 1965 and signalling a shift from ad hoc engagement to institutionalised sector partnerships. The package included counter‑terrorism and defence education pacts, twin‑destination tourism promotion and university links, alongside reciprocal land allocation to house each other’s missions in Colombo and Malé, an indicator of long‑term diplomatic intent rather than transactional diplomacy. Trade was framed as a priority: among South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation markets, the island neighbour ranks as one of the larger buyers of Sri Lankan goods, and a possible entry by Bank of Maldives was flagged as a way to ease payments and deepen commercial flows that have historically moved through third countries. Maritime cooperation featured prominently because both economies rely on Indian Ocean shipping lanes; agreements to curb illicit use of sea routes and to explore fisheries processing and boat‑building tie into existing domestic value chains rather than creating parallel ones. Climate advocacy was elevated to a regional plank, with both governments committing to use multilateral forums to press for finance and adaptation reflective of small‑island exposure to sea‑level rise and weather shocks. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#1-sri-lanka-and-the-maldives-signed-seven-cooperation-agreements-covering-defence-education-tourism-health-and-archives
Consumer prices in Colombo accelerated to 5.4% year‑on‑year in April, up from 2.2% in March. The jump, measured by the Colombo Consumer Price Index, was driven by a sharp rise in non‑food items to 6.8% and a pickup in food to 2.8%, reversing three consecutive months of sub‑3% readings and pushing the month‑on‑month change to 2.99% after a 0.25% increase previously. The Central Bank noted the outcome stays within its 5% target band of 3–7%, but flagged elevated uncertainty from the Middle East war that has tightened global energy markets and filtered into transport costs locally. Core inflation rose to 2.8% from 2.5%, indicating underlying pressures had begun to firm from the near‑zero trough seen in late 2025. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#2-consumer-prices-in-colombo-accelerated-to-54-yearonyear-in-april-up-from-22-in-march
Parliament received a Bill to widen Value Added Tax, lower the registration threshold and tax cross‑border digital services from July. The proposal reduces compulsory registration to Rs. 36 million annually and Rs. 9 million per quarter—from Rs. 60 million and Rs. 15 million—bringing more small and medium traders into the net while raising the financial services VAT to 20.5% from 18%, increasing the sector’s tax burden relative to last year. Overseas platforms supplying services to users in the country must register and charge VAT, except on supplies to VAT‑registered businesses supported by documentation, aligning the treatment of foreign sellers with domestic providers. Retailers are required to move to secured point‑of‑sale systems on a timetable set by the Inland Revenue, a compliance step that is expected to improve invoice traceability compared with manual receipts. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#3-parliament-received-a-bill-to-widen-value-added-tax-lower-the-registration-threshold-and-tax-crossborder-digital-services-from-july
The Asian Development Bank cut its 2026 growth outlook for developing Asia to 4.7% and lifted inflation to 5.2%. The revisions—down from 5.1% growth and 3% price gains—were attributed to sustained energy and freight disruption from the Gulf conflict, with oil assumed to average about $96 per barrel this year versus sub‑$70 levels earlier in the year. Under a worse‑case escalation where critical energy facilities suffer more damage, the institution projected growth as low as 4.2% and inflation as high as 7.4%, a combination that would squeeze import‑dependent economies with limited fiscal buffers compared to 2025. The lender urged targeted, time‑bound support to vulnerable households instead of broad subsidies, a shift from blanket reliefs that previously widened deficits without improving energy efficiency. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#4-the-asian-development-bank-cut-its-2026-growth-outlook-for-developing-asia-to-47-and-lifted-inflation-to-52
Sri Lanka rose five places to 134th in the 2026 World Press Freedom Index published by Reporters Without Borders. The improvement from 139th a year earlier still left the media environment classed as problematic, with concerns documented over national security laws and the Online Safety Commission created in January 2024, whose powers include content takedowns and access to sources that can chill reporting compared with peer jurisdictions with stronger whistle‑blower protection. The index noted more than half of countries globally fell into the difficult or very serious categories, a deterioration versus 2002 when the ranking began, underscoring that incremental gains domestically occurred against a broadly worsening backdrop rather than a global rebound. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#5-sri-lanka-rose-five-places-to-134th-in-the-2026-world-press-freedom-index-published-by-reporters-without-borders
A committee proposed legal and operational mechanisms to enable out‑of‑country voting for an estimated 1.5 million citizens abroad. The report to the Public Administration Ministry set out voter registration via missions, campaign rules under the Election Expenditure Act, and options for in‑person balloting at embassies, postal voting or secure online systems, a package to be taken to Cabinet after consultations instead of piloting ad hoc arrangements as in past exercises. The draft also encouraged exploring advance or mobile voting domestically for essential services personnel and bedridden voters, extending access beyond the diaspora relative to prior cycles. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#6-a-committee-proposed-legal-and-operational-mechanisms-to-enable-outofcountry-voting-for-an-estimated-15-million-citizens-abroad
Rules empowering the Central Bank to compel statistics from external parties were approved for submission to Parliament, alongside its 2025 report. The gazetted framework under the 2023 Act clarifies collection scope, confidentiality and administrative sanctions for non‑compliance, replacing scattered directives with a single rulebook so that monetary policy and financial stability work are not hampered by delayed or incomplete data compared with the ad hoc requests used previously. The annual operational and audited financial statements for last year were cleared for tabling, keeping statutory timelines intact after the governance overhaul that split monetary and fiscal roles more explicitly than under the old law. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#7-rules-empowering-the-central-bank-to-compel-statistics-from-external-parties-were-approved-for-submission-to-parliament-alongside-its-2025-report
The International Finance Corporation agreed to finance a 100‑megawatt solar project with WindForce via a local‑currency loan of up to $18 million. The investment, supported by the IDA Private Sector Window Local Currency Facility, will help build Sri Lanka’s first utility‑scale photovoltaic plant with provision for future battery storage, adding an estimated 220 gigawatt‑hours a year to the grid and creating more than 3,000 jobs, according to the lender. Advisory support on engineering, grid resilience and cybersecurity accompanies the financing, complementing the World Bank’s renewable integration program rather than operating as a standalone asset. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#8-the-international-finance-corporation-agreed-to-finance-a-100megawatt-solar-project-with-windforce-via-a-localcurrency-loan-of-up-to-18-million
Russia’s Health Minister began a three‑day visit for talks on pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, training and academic links. The delegation’s programme included meetings with counterparts from health and foreign ministries, tours of the National Hospital and the University of Colombo’s medical faculty, and discussions on Ayurveda and health tourism, a sectoral agenda that has focused on functional cooperation rather than treaty‑level commitments in past exchanges. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#9-russias-health-minister-began-a-threeday-visit-for-talks-on-pharmaceuticals-medical-equipment-training-and-academic-links
Police detained more than 120 foreign nationals in Rajagiriya over suspected financial fraud operations spanning multiple floors of an apartment complex. Laptops and mobile phones were seized for forensic analysis, with the group including citizens of China, Vietnam, Malaysia, Madagascar, the Philippines, Thailand and Cambodia; several suspects fled during the raid and are being sought, an enforcement action that follows earlier cases in which transnational cyber‑scam cells used short‑term rentals instead of registered offices. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#10-police-detained-more-than-120-foreign-nationals-in-rajagiriya-over-suspected-financial-fraud-operations-spanning-multiple-floors-of-an-apartment-complex
Construction activity remained in expansion in March with a Total Activity Index of 57.1, down from 70.3 in February. New orders and employment continued to grow but at a slower pace, while suppliers’ delivery times lengthened notably amid transport bottlenecks linked to the Middle East conflict and reported difficulties sourcing fuel and inputs; expectations for the next quarter stayed above 50, reflecting a pipeline of work even as costs and logistics stiffened compared to early‑year conditions. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#11-construction-activity-remained-in-expansion-in-march-with-a-total-activity-index-of-571-down-from-703-in-february
The head of state convened a high‑level review on introducing electric suburban rail services on three key corridors around the capital. The first phase under the Colombo Suburban Railway Project would run on the Kelani Valley, Main Line to Ragama, and Coastal Line to Panadura, with funding options, infrastructure upgrades and a possible shift from broad to standard gauge examined, a move that would modernise commuter capacity relative to the current diesel fleet if procurement and rights‑of‑way are secured on schedule. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#12-the-head-of-state-convened-a-highlevel-review-on-introducing-electric-suburban-rail-services-on-three-key-corridors-around-the-capital
Maldives’ president arrived for a two‑day State visit that included a ceremonial welcome, bilateral talks and a business forum in Colombo. The programme featured the exchange of cooperation documents across several sectors and discussions on trade imbalance and investor entry, building on a prior visit by Sri Lanka’s leadership to Malé in 2025 rather than inaugurating ties from scratch; the itinerary also underscored plans to improve air and maritime links that underpin twin‑destination tourism compared with single‑market itineraries. Tue, 05 May 2026 11:55:43 +0530 https://github.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/blob/main/data/readme_history/README.20260505.md#13-maldives-president-arrived-for-a-twoday-state-visit-that-included-a-ceremonial-welcome-bilateral-talks-and-a-business-forum-in-colombo >>>>>>> 3b7f21c (Update RSS feed and add news digest for May 5, 2026)
